heading: Purchase of office equipment or furnishings by Senators
label: 6320 Purchase of office equipment or furnishings by Senators
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 59b of this title

children.heading: Authorization; conditions
children.label: a Authorization; conditions
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a United States Senator may purchase, upon leaving office or otherwise ceasing to be a Senator (except by expulsion), any item or items of office equipment or office furnishings provided by the General Services Administration and then currently located and in use in an office of such Senator in the State then represented by such Senator.

children.heading: Request by Senator and arrangement for purchase by Sergeant at Arms of Senate; regulations governing purchase; price
children.label: b Request by Senator and arrangement for purchase by Sergeant at Arms of Senate; regulations governing purchase; price

children.text: in accordance with regulations which shall be prescribed by the Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate, after consultation with the General Services Administration; and

at a price equal to the acquisition cost to the Federal Government of the equipment or furnishings so purchased, less allowance for depreciation determined under such regulations, but in no instance less than the fair market value of such items.

children.heading: Remittance of amounts received to General Services Administration; disposition
children.label: c Remittance of amounts received to General Services Administration; disposition
children.text: Amounts received by the Federal Government from the sale of items of office equipment or office furnishings under this section shall be remitted to the General Services Administration and credited to the appropriate account or accounts.
